assertions.reason: The dedicated browser session could not complete a wallet connection. MetaMask never produced a connected account or chain badge, Base Account redirected to Coinbase onboarding outside the app, Rainbow never connected, and WalletConnect emitted unauthorized/invalid-key errors.

steps.action: Open http://localhost:5173/ and click Connect Wallet
steps.expected: RainbowKit modal opens with connect options
steps.observed: Modal opened with Rainbow, Base Account, MetaMask, and WalletConnect options

steps.expected: Wallet connects and the UI shows a connected address on chain ID 420420417
steps.observed: The modal stayed open, MetaMask became disabled, and no connected address or chain badge appeared

steps.action: Try alternate wallet options within the same session
steps.expected: An available browser wallet path connects if MetaMask is unavailable
steps.observed: Base Account redirected to https://keys.coinbase.com/onboarding with Sign in/Create account, Rainbow did not progress, and WalletConnect stayed in the modal while browser errors reported WebSocket code 3000 Unauthorized: invalid key

evidence.consoleErrors: Local dev build emitted Reown/WalletConnect 403/400 remote-config errors; WalletConnect attempts logged repeated 'WebSocket connection closed abnormally with code: 3000 (Unauthorized: invalid key)' and 'Connection interrupted while trying to subscribe'.
network: Browser loaded http://localhost:5173/ successfully before connect attempts
WalletConnect/Reown configuration requests failed with HTTP 403/400 as reported by the browser console

assertions.issues: Blocked by missing injected/importable wallet in the isolated browser session plus a broken WalletConnect fallback configuration.

assertions.reason: Deposit-collateral testing could not start because the page never reached a connected-wallet state. The Write Path section remained an empty state reading 'Connect your wallet to access lending operations.'

steps.action: Scroll to the Write Path section on the local current-code build
steps.expected: Connected-wallet deposit controls are available after wallet connection
steps.observed: The section showed only the empty-state message because no wallet connected

steps.action: Attempt to connect a wallet first
steps.expected: Proceed to wrap PAS -> approve WPAS -> deposit collateral
steps.observed: Wallet connection was blocked before any wrap/approve/deposit form could be exercised

evidence.consoleErrors: No form-specific errors were reachable because the connected state was never entered; same wallet-connect diagnostics as VAL-UI-002 applied.
network: No lending write transaction was submitted because the wallet prerequisite never completed

assertions.reason: Borrow testing was blocked by the same missing connected-wallet prerequisite; no borrow form became usable in the browser session.

steps.expected: Borrow form is available after connection
steps.observed: Only the empty-state message was visible

steps.expected: Connected state reveals the borrow form and allows submission
steps.observed: No wallet connection completed, so no borrow submission or USDC balance confirmation was possible

evidence.consoleErrors: Same wallet-connect diagnostics as VAL-UI-002; no borrow-specific errors were reachable.
network: No borrow transaction or Blockscout tx link was generated

assertions.reason: Repay testing was blocked because the browser session never obtained a connected wallet and therefore never exposed the repay form.

steps.expected: Repay form is available for a connected wallet
steps.observed: The section stayed in the disconnected empty state

steps.expected: Connection succeeds and repay can be submitted
steps.observed: Connection did not complete, so no debt decrease or repayment transaction could be observed

evidence.consoleErrors: Same wallet-connect diagnostics as VAL-UI-002; no repay-specific errors were reachable.
network: No repay transaction or explorer link was generated

assertions.reason: Liquidation testing was blocked before a borrower address or repayment amount could be submitted because the page never reached a connected-wallet state.

steps.action: Use the local current-code write surface
steps.expected: Liquidate form is available after connection
steps.observed: Only the disconnected empty state was visible

steps.action: Attempt to connect a wallet in the same session
steps.expected: A connected wallet can attempt liquidation against a target borrower
steps.observed: No wallet connected, so no liquidation submission or tx confirmation could be attempted

evidence.consoleErrors: Same wallet-connect diagnostics as VAL-UI-002; no liquidation-specific errors were reachable.
network: No liquidation transaction or explorer link was generated

assertions.reason: Pending/confirmed transaction banners and Blockscout tx links could not be validated because no write transaction was ever submitted from the browser.

steps.action: Attempt to reach any browser write flow
steps.expected: Submitting a form shows pending then confirmed state with a Blockscout link
steps.observed: No form submission was possible because the wallet prerequisite never completed

network: No transaction hash, pending banner, or confirmed explorer link was produced

assertions.reason: Withdraw-collateral testing was blocked because the browser session never exposed connected-wallet write forms.

steps.expected: Withdraw form appears after wallet connection
steps.observed: The section stayed in the disconnected empty state
No wallet connected, so no collateral decrease or confirmation state could be observed

evidence.consoleErrors: Same wallet-connect diagnostics as VAL-UI-002; no withdraw-specific errors were reachable.
network: No withdraw transaction or explorer link was generated

assertions.reason: Supply-liquidity testing was blocked by the same connection prerequisite; the supply form never became accessible in the browser session.

steps.expected: Supply-liquidity form appears after wallet connection
steps.observed: The section showed only the disconnected empty state

steps.expected: Connected state allows a USDC-test supply transaction
steps.observed: No wallet connected, so no supply transaction or LP share confirmation could be observed

evidence.consoleErrors: Same wallet-connect diagnostics as VAL-UI-002; no supply-specific errors were reachable.
network: No supply transaction or explorer link was generated

assertions.reason: The full browser lending lifecycle could not be validated because the very first prerequisite, wallet connection, failed in the isolated session. Without a connected wallet, none of the deposit/borrow/repay/reborrow steps or post-write market refreshes could be exercised.

steps.action: Connect a wallet on the local current-code frontend
steps.expected: A browser wallet connects on Polkadot Hub TestNet and unlocks the write lifecycle
steps.observed: No wallet option produced a connected session

steps.action: Proceed through deposit -> borrow -> wait -> repay partial -> borrow more
steps.expected: Lifecycle completes and the market snapshot/recent activity refreshes after each successful write
steps.observed: Lifecycle could not begin because the write forms never became available

network: No successful write transaction was submitted, so no post-write snapshot or recent-activity refresh could be observed

assertions.issues: Blocked by the unresolved wallet/connectivity prerequisites described in VAL-UI-002.

frictions.description: The hosted fallback at http://eyawa.me/dualvm-lending/ loaded, but it is an older observer-only build and did not expose a visible Connect Wallet button or write-path controls.
frictions.resolution: Used the hosted URL only to confirm it was unsuitable as a write-path fallback, then returned to the local current-code URL for all assigned assertions.

frictions.description: The local current-code page copy is internally inconsistent: the page advertises a browser write path, but other visible copy still describes the UI as observer-first/intentionally observer-only.
frictions.resolution: Recorded the contradiction and relied on observed behavior rather than marketing copy.

frictions.description: The Base Account option in RainbowKit immediately redirected to Coinbase Keys onboarding, which requires an external account flow not mentioned in the test guidance.
frictions.resolution: Recorded the off-app redirect as evidence, then returned to the app and continued testing the remaining wallet options in the same session.

blockers.description: No importable/injected browser wallet was available inside the single isolated agent-browser session. The MetaMask path did not establish a connected account and the task constraints did not permit creating a new external wallet account.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried MetaMask, Rainbow, Base Account, and WalletConnect within the same session; none produced a connected wallet.

blockers.description: WalletConnect/Reown configuration is broken in the local build for this session. Console diagnostics showed remote-config HTTP 403/400 failures and repeated WebSocket code 3000 Unauthorized: invalid key errors.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried the WalletConnect option after reopening the app in the same session; the same unauthorized/invalid-key errors recurred.

blockers.description: The hosted fallback deployment could not serve as a write-path backup because it lacked a visible connect control and remained observer-only.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Opened the hosted URL in the same browser session and confirmed it was not usable for the assigned write-path assertions.

summary: The local current-code build exposed a Connect Wallet button and the disconnected Write Path section, but no wallet option produced a usable connected session in the isolated browser. MetaMask did not connect, Base redirected to Coinbase onboarding, WalletConnect failed with unauthorized invalid-key errors, and the hosted fallback was observer-only, so all assigned write-path assertions remained blocked.
